name: PdfDing
description: Selfhosted PDF manager, viewer and editor offering a seamless user experience on multiple devices.
readme_summary: PdfDing is a self-hosted PDF management, viewing, and editing application designed for seamless multi-device use. It offers features like workspaces, tagging, highlighting, and annotation, with a focus on a clean UI and easy Docker-based setup.
key_features: Browser-based PDF viewing with position memory
Workspaces, collections, multi-level tagging, starring, archiving
Clean UI with dark mode and customization
Share PDFs with external access control
primary_use_case: Self-hosted management, viewing, and editing of PDF documents across multiple devices.
cover_image_prompt: A librarian meticulously organizing a vast, multi-tiered library filled with glowing PDF books. Each book has a small bookmark indicating its reading progress. The librarian uses a magical quill to add annotations and highlights directly onto the book covers. In the background, a subtle digital interface displays collection tags and workspace icons. The overall scene is a blend of a cozy, traditional library and a modern digital hub, bathed in soft, ambient light. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, organized aesthetic, emphasizing the seamless organization and interactive editing capabilities of PdfDing.

name: copilot-sdk
description: Multi-platform SDK for integrating GitHub Copilot Agent into apps and services
readme_summary: The GitHub Copilot SDK provides a set of libraries for multiple programming languages (Python, TypeScript, Go, .NET, Java, Rust) that allow developers to integrate GitHub Copilot's agentic workflows directly into their applications and services. It exposes the core engine of Copilot CLI, enabling programmatic invocation of agent behaviors, including planning, tool usage, and file modifications, without requiring developers to build their own orchestration logic.
key_features: Multi-platform SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Go, .NET, Java, Rust)
Integrates GitHub Copilot Agent into applications
Handles planning, tool invocation, and file edits
No need for custom orchestration logic
primary_use_case: Enabling developers to embed GitHub Copilot's AI agent capabilities into their own applications and services.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a bustling workshop, using a magical blueprint to guide robotic arms that are assembling intricate, glowing components. The blueprint displays abstract code and data streams, representing the AI's plan. The robotic arms are shown precisely placing and connecting parts, symbolizing the SDK's ability to orchestrate complex tasks like tool invocation and file edits. Subtle UI elements showing progress and success indicators are visible on a nearby workbench. The overall scene is vibrant and futuristic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, organized composition and a palette of blues, purples, and glowing whites, with the 'GitHub Copilot SDK' subtly etched onto a tool.

name: Vibe-Trading
readme_summary: Vibe-Trading is a Python-based personal trading agent designed to empower users with comprehensive trading capabilities. It leverages multi-agent systems, AI, and LLMs for algorithmic trading, backtesting, and portfolio management, offering a flexible and powerful platform for quantitative finance.
key_features: Shadow Account for risk-free strategy testing
primary_use_case: To provide an intelligent, automated, and comprehensive platform for individuals and developers to engage in algorithmic trading, strategy development, and backtesting using advanced AI and multi-agent techniques.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, anthropomorphic owl wearing a monocle and a small, sophisticated vest, perched on a stack of glowing data cubes. The owl is actively directing a swarm of smaller, diligent robotic birds, each carrying a financial chart or a buy/sell signal. These birds are flying from a chaotic cloud of raw stock tickers and data streams towards a neatly organized, illuminated vault labeled 'Vibe-Trading'. Subtle UI elements showing trading graphs and code snippets are integrated into the data cubes. The scene is set in a futuristic, yet cozy, library with warm, ambient lighting, emphasizing intelligence and control. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a vibrant, yet professional, color palette.
